Patch Set 1:

Would this also affect truncate operations?  Just curious, since you added the parallel task framework I've been thinking about using it to speed up truncate, because truncates of a many-striped file are serialized in LDLM, waiting for the lock from each stripe.  (So the time required scales linearly with the number of stripes.)

Parallelizing it would obviously be helpful

Patch Set 1:

Does this replace https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/24475/ ?

Patch Set 1:

Yes, this is other version of parallel I/O. If this approach is better I will abandon https://review.whamcloud.com/24475.

Patch Set 1:

Current version will not affect truncate operations.

parents.subject: LU-8964 libcfs: Introduce parallel tasks framework

commit.subject: LU-8964 clio: Parallelize generic I/O
commit.message:
LU-8964 clio: Parallelize generic I/O

Add parallel version of cl_io_loop() function which use information
about stripes from LOV layer and process them in parallel.

Rename struct cl_io_rw_common into struct cl_io_range and remove
mostly unused crw_nonblock member. This should reduce the size of
struct cl_io.

The counter ci_owned_nr is checked for positive numbers but it was
defined as unsigned.

Signed-off-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Change-Id: I56e90b13fd19f0df0cf0ec189386c95fc374762d
